🚨| Major Franco Colapinto backer makes sudden F1 return:

— Alpine has confirmed the renewal of its agreement with a major Franco Colapinto sponsor, Mercado Libre, just before the F1 season begins with the Australian Grand Prix. Speaking to media, Colapinto expressed his gratitude for the continued support:

“Starting my third year in Formula 1 is both a huge challenge and a dream come true. Each season demands more preparation, more focus, and more ambition, and having the support of Mercado Libre, which believed in me from the very beginning, gives all that effort a very special meaning.”

“Today, I feel more experienced and more confident to push forward in this new chapter, ready to start the season in Australia and give everything from the very first lap.”

— Flavio Briatore, Alpine executive adviser, emphasized the importance of the partnership with Mercado Libre:

“We are proud to continue our partnership with Mercado Libre this season. From the beginning, we found a shared vision based on innovation, ambition, and the relentless pursuit of high performance. Their presence strengthens our connection with Latin America, a key region for Formula 1, and we deeply value the company's sustained commitment to our project and to the talent that is part of the team.”

— Sean Summers, EVP and CMO of Mercado Libre, reflected on the journey with Colapinto:

“Three years ago, we decided to back a young talent who was taking his first steps in Formula 1. There were no guarantees, no shortcuts. From day one, we were impressed by his work ethic, his character, and his enormous ambition to reach the highest level. We immediately knew that was where we wanted to be.”

“Today, seeing Franco start his third year in the category, firmly established within Alpine, ready to take on new challenges and truly compete against the best in the world, is a tremendous source of pride for all of us who have followed and supported him from the very beginning.”

“With this continued support for Franco, Mercado Libre completes its commitment to Latin American talent in Formula 1, and we begin the new season with great enthusiasm.”

Sirve para monitorear ofertas rápido, comparar entre productos o sitios y encontrar lo más barato. No compra nada solo (eso lo hacés vos), pero sí te ayuda a cazar deals mejores y evitar pagar de más. En este caso mostró que logueado te clavan más caro.

Mercado Libre opens its first fulfillment center in China

Mercado Libre has opened its first fulfillment center in China, shipping products to its five main Latin American markets in a direct response to the growing competition from Chinese platforms like Temu and Shein in the region.

The hub is part of a broader strategy to deepen the  China-Latin America trade corridor and scale a fulfilled-from-China model that aims to expand product variety while improving pricing and service levels.

The company estimates the cross-border e-commerce market in Latin America at around US$10 billion, a segment where it sees significant room to grow among its 121 million unique active buyers.

Mercado Libre also launched a digital advertising platform for Chinese advertisers.
